seoTitle: How to Change Your First Name on TurboTax
metaDescription: Update your first name in TurboTax after a marriage, divorce, or legal name change so your tax documents process correctly with the IRS.
intro: Whether it's from marriage, divorce, or a personal preference, keeping your name updated in TurboTax matters for accurate tax filings and IRS compliance. This guide walks through navigating the interface, updating your name, and saving the change. It's built for anyone who needs to update this basic but important piece of personal information, whether on desktop or mobile.

steps.heading: Navigate to your TurboTax profile
steps.body: Find the settings area of TurboTax where your personal information, including your name, is managed.

steps.heading: Update your name
steps.body: Enter and save your new first name in the appropriate field within TurboTax.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Confirm the update so your new name is reflected across your TurboTax account and documents.

steps.heading: Understand why accurate information matters
steps.body: Keeping your name updated helps ensure your tax documents are processed correctly and avoids complications with the IRS.

faq.question: Does changing my name in TurboTax also update it with the IRS or Social Security Administration?
faq.answer: No, updating your name in TurboTax only changes it within the platform; you'll need to update your legal name with the Social Security Administration separately for it to match your tax filings.

faq.question: Can I change my name from a mobile device?
faq.answer: Yes, this guide covers making the update whether you're using a desktop or mobile device.

faq.question: Will an outdated name cause my return to be rejected?
faq.answer: A mismatch between your TurboTax name and IRS records can cause processing issues, so it's worth confirming both are aligned before filing.

faq.question: Is this useful for business owners as well as individuals?
faq.answer: Yes, this tutorial is made for everyone from first-time TurboTax users to seasoned professionals who need to keep their personal information accurate.
